Problem Set: Unit 2: Applications of Differentiation 2C-1
Cut four identical squares of side x out of the corners of a 12 × 12 inch piece of cardboard and fold the sides up to make a box without a top. Find the size of the corner square that maximizes the volume of the box.

Problem Set: Unit 2: Applications of Differentiation 2E-4
Sand is pouring onto a conical pile at a rate of 12 m³/min, in such a way that the diameter of the base is always 3/2 the height. Find the rate at which the height is increasing when the pile is 2 m tall.

Problem Set: Unit 3: Integration3C-2a
 Calculate the definite integral ∫₀² √(3x + 5) dx.

Problem Set: Unit 3: Integration 3F-2a
Solve the differential equation dy/dx = 4xy with the initial condition y(1) = 3. Find y(3).

Problem Set: Unit 4: Applications of Integration 4A-4
 Find the area between y = sin x and y = cos x from one crossing to the next

Problem Set: Unit 5: Integration Techniques 5C-1
Evaluate ∫ sin²(x) dx.

Problem Set: Unit 6: Additional Topics 6A-1a
Find the limit: lim(x→0) sin(3x)/x.

Problem Set: Unit 2: Applications of Differentiation 2C-4
The U.S. Postal Service accepts boxes whose length plus girth equals at most 108 inches. What are the dimensions of the accepted box of largest volume, and what is its volume in cubic feet? ("Length" is the longest dimension and "girth" is the perimeter of the perpendicular face. Note: the best shape for the face is a square.)
column4: Dimensions: 36 in × 18 in × 18 in; Volume = 6.75 cubic feet

Problem Set: Unit 2: Applications of Differentiation 2E-7
A trough is filled with water at a rate of 1 cubic meter per second. The trough has a trapezoidal cross-section with a lower base of half a meter, one-meter sides opening outward at 45° from the base, and a length of 4 meters. What is the rate at which the water level h is rising when h is one half meter?
Problem Set: Unit 3: Integration 3C-3a Calculate the definite integral ∫₁² x dx/(x² + 1).

Find the volume of the solid of revolution generated by rotating the region bounded by y = 1 − x², y = 0 around the x-axis.
Problem Set: Unit 5: Integration Techniques 5E-1 Evaluate the integral ∫ dx / ((x − 2)(x + 3)).

Problem Set: Unit 5: Integration Techniques 5F-2a
